content:
3 min read
Just now

--

You’ve seen the headlines. GPT-4o dropped its price again. Gemini is practically free. Building AI products has never been cheaper — or so the story goes.
Then your first real invoice arrives.
I’ve talked to dozens of founders who budgeted carefully for their LLM API costs and still got blindsided. Not because they miscalculated the AI part. Because they forgot everything else.
Here’s what actually shows up on your bill when you ship an AI product.

1. The LLM cost (the one everyone plans for)
Yes, this one is obvious. But even here, the variance is enormous.
For a typical SaaS app processing 50,000 user requests per month with ~800 tokens average per request:
ModelMonthly CostGPT-4o~$200/moGPT-4o mini~$11/moClaude 3.5 Haiku~$9/moGemini 1.5 Flash~$6/mo
That’s a 33x difference. Most early-stage products don’t need GPT-4o. They reach for it by default.
Before writing a line of production code, run your numbers through a free LLM cost calculator — it takes 2 minutes and saves real money.

2. The vector database bill (the RAG tax)
Almost every AI product built today uses Retrieval-Augmented Generation. That means a vector database.
Here’s what founders discover too late: vector DB pricing is nothing like regular database pricing. You’re paying for storage, query units, and often pod uptime — simultaneously.
A typical early-stage RAG app with 500K vectors and 100K queries/month:

• Pinecone (Starter → Standard): $70–$140/mo

• Weaviate Cloud: $25–$60/mo

• Qdrant Cloud: $9–$25/mo
The vector database cost calculator lets you compare these side by side with your actual numbers. Most founders who use it switch providers.

3. Authentication (the “just use Clerk” trap)
Auth feels free until you scale. Every major auth provider has a free tier that runs out right around the time you’re celebrating your first 1,000 users.

• Clerk: Free up to 10,000 MAU, then $25/mo — but enterprise features add up fast

• Auth0: Free up to 7,500 MAU, then pricing jumps significantly

• Supabase Auth: Included in database plan, very competitive at scale

• WorkOS: Designed for B2B, expensive but enterprise-ready
The auth provider cost calculator shows your break-even point between providers at your projected user count.

4. Serverless function costs (the invisible meter)
AI apps are compute-heavy. Every LLM call, every embedding generation, every RAG retrieval runs in a function somewhere.
AWS Lambda and Vercel Functions sound cheap until your AI app is running 2-second inference calls at scale.

• Lambda: $0.0000166667 per GB-second. A 2GB function running 2 seconds = $0.0000667 per call. At 1M calls/mo: $66/mo

• Vercel Functions: More predictable, but the Pro plan caps and overages surprise founders
Use the serverless cost calculator to model this before you architect your backend.

5. Payment processing (if you’re charging for AI)
Most AI products monetize with usage-based or subscription pricing. Your payment processor takes a cut of everything.

• Stripe: 2.9% + $0.30 per transaction

• Paddle: 5% + $0.50 (but handles global VAT/tax for you)

• Lemon Squeezy: Similar to Paddle
At $10,000 MRR, the difference between Stripe and Paddle is ~$210/month. At $100K MRR, it’s real money — but Paddle’s tax handling might be worth it depending on your markets.
The payment processor fee calculator shows the real dollar difference at your revenue level.

6. The cost nobody mentions: prompt caching
This one goes the other direction — it’s money you’re leaving on the table.
If your app uses a long system prompt (most do), and you’re not using prompt caching, you’re paying full price for the same tokens on every single request.
Anthropic and OpenAI both offer caching. For a 2,000-token system prompt at 1M requests/month:

• Without caching: ~$6,000/mo in input tokens alone

• With caching: ~$600/mo
That’s $5,400/month in savings from one configuration change. The prompt caching calculator shows your exact savings.

The real number
Add it all up for a typical early-stage AI SaaS:
ComponentMonthly CostLLM API$50–$500Vector DB$25–$140Auth$0–$100Serverless$20–$200Payment processing3–5% of revenueTotal$100–$1,000+
Before you hit any meaningful revenue.
The founders who survive are the ones who model this before they build — not after their first AWS bill arrives at 2am.

All calculators referenced in this article are free to use at apicalculators.com. No signup required.
